title: Understanding Fractions
description: This arc teaches the fundamentals of fractions using visual models and real-world contexts, focusing on comparing, adding, and subtracting like fractions.

stages.title: Hook
stages.description: Introduction to fractions through engaging stories and visuals.

problems.text: You and a friend are sharing a pizza. The pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. You eat 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza did you eat?

stages.title: Concept
stages.description: Learn the meaning of fractions using visual models.

problems.text: Draw a rectangle and shade 1/4 of it.
problems.solution: Visual representation; one out of four equal parts shaded

problems.text: What fraction is represented by 3 shaded parts out of 5 equal parts?
A garden is divided into 10 equal sections. Rosa planted flowers in 3 sections. What fraction of the garden did Rosa plant?

stages.title: Practice
stages.description: Independent practice problems to solidify understanding.

problems.text: A recipe needs 3/8 cup of sugar. Mia has already added 1/8 cup. How much more sugar does she still need?
Sara ate 3/8 of a pizza. Tom ate 4/8 of the same pizza. Who ate more?
problems.solution: Tom ate more because 4 > 3 when denominators are equal

problems.text: A garden is divided into 10 equal sections. Rosa planted flowers in 3 sections. Her brother planted in 4 sections. What fraction of the garden is planted altogether?

stages.title: Capstone
stages.description: Challenging, multi-part problem that integrates all previous learning.

problems.text: A recipe needs 3/8 cup of sugar. Mia has already added 1/8 cup. How much more sugar does she still need to add? And what fraction of the total sugar has she added so far?
solution: 2/8 cup more needed to complete the recipe
1/8 of the total sugar has been added so far

guiding_question.text: What's the total amount of sugar the recipe needs? And how much has Mia put in already? Start with those two pieces.

review_mode.description: Once Stage 5 is passed, the child enters Review mode for this topic. In future sessions, Nova surfaces one Stage 4–5 level problem per session for this topic. No hook, no concept — just the problem, as a warm-up or closing challenge.

misconceptions.title: Flip numerator/denominator
misconceptions.nova_approach: You have the right numbers — which is the total, which is your part?

misconceptions.title: Add denominators
misconceptions.nova_approach: The garden still has 10 sections. Does planting more change the total?

misconceptions.title: Compare fractions by numerator only without checking denominator
Use remaining parts instead of taken parts
misconceptions.nova_approach: The question asks how much she ATE — which number is that?
